name: good-commit-messages
description: Use when committing changes, to write clear conventional commit messages
instructions: Write commit messages in the conventional commits style: a short type prefix (feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore) followed by a concise imperative-mood summary under 72 characters, e.g. "fix: handle empty project_root path". If the change is non-trivial, add a blank line and a short body explaining why the change was made, not just what changed. Never include the word "AI" or mention that the commit was AI-assisted in the message itself. Always run git_diff first to see what is actually being committed before writing the message, rather than guessing from the conversation alone.
